steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content:
The function is
$$
f(x)=(x-1)^2+7
$$
This is vertex form, and the squared part can never be negative.

steps.title: Make the square zero
steps.content:
The smallest possible value of $(x-1)^2$ is $0$. That happens when $x=1$, so the minimum value of the function is
$$
0+7=7
$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing the function shows the vertex at $(1,7)$, so the minimum value is $7$. The answer is C.
